name: Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop
name_original: Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles – circular por la sierra de Monsalud y de Rozallana
description: Explore the Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles, a moderate 18.0-mile mountain biking route with scenic singletracks and cork oa
region_intro: Mountain biking around Nogales offers a diverse landscape, characterized by "sky islands," wooded hills, and waving grasslands that rise from the desert floor. The region features several mountain ranges, including the Santa Rita Mountains and Tumacacori Highlands, providing varied terrain. Many mountain bike trails are found within the Nogales Ranger District of the Coronado National Forest, offering a mix of climbs and descents. This unique environment provides a rich backdrop for outdoor activities. Best mountain bike trails around Nogales The most popular mountain bike trail is Castillo de Nogales a Castillo de Salvatierra — circular desde Torre de Miguel Sesmero, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers expansive views of the surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torre de Miguel Sesmero a Salvaleón — circular por el Castillo de Nogales, a moderate 22.0 miles (35.4 km) path. This trail winds through varied terrain, including sections near historic sites and wooded areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop, a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail leading through rolling hills and open landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Nogales is defined by "sky island" mountains, wooded hills, and grasslands. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Nogales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Nogales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Nogales?
A: Nogales offers a wide selection of mountain biking opportunities, with over 80 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents and descents.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on mountain bike trails in Nogales?
A: The terrain around Nogales is surprisingly diverse, characterized by unique "sky islands," wooded hills, and waving grasslands that rise from the desert floor. You'll find trails within the Nogales Ranger District of the Coronado National Forest, offering a mix of climbs and descents through mountainous areas like the Santa Rita Mountains and Tumacacori Highlands.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Nogales?
A: Yes, Nogales has several easy mountain bike trails. While specific beginner-only routes aren't highlighted, many moderate trails offer accessible sections. For a generally moderate experience that can be enjoyed by those looking for less technical rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359926406/nogales-reservoir-and-12-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Reservoir and 12 Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 16.4 miles (26.4 km) with manageable elevation changes.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Nogales offers 16 difficult mountain bike trails. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359071536/castillo-de-nogales-a-castillo-de-salvatierra-circular-desde-torre-de-miguel-sesmero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales a Castillo de Salvatierra — circular desde Torre de Miguel Sesmero&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.3-mile (45.5 km) trail with significant elevation gain, provide a robust workout and technical sections through varied terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with scenic views around Nogales?
A: Absolutely. The "sky island" landscape and surrounding mountain ranges like the Santa Rita Mountains provide numerous opportunities for scenic views. Many trails, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030784911/route-of-the-twelve-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, lead through rolling hills and open landscapes, offering expansive vistas of the unique Arizona environment.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass by or are near significant natural features and landmarks. You might encounter ancient cork oaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3272177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Twelve Apostles (Centenary Cork Oaks)&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2815838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra María Andrés Special Area of Conservation&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2779557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Cliffs of Risco de Valbellido&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the mountain biking routes?
A: Yes, the area around Nogales is rich in history. You can find attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which some routes pass by, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Battle of Albuera Memorial Mosaic&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358790340/torre-de-miguel-sesmero-a-salvaleon-circular-por-el-castillo-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Miguel Sesmero a Salvaleón — circular por el Castillo de Nogales&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, winds through areas with historical significance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Nogales?
A: The mountain biking trails in Nogales are highly rated by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the unique "sky island" landscapes, and the mix of challenging climbs and rewarding descents that make for an engaging experience.

Q: Are there loop mountain bike trails available in Nogales?
A: Loop trails are a popular option in Nogales, allowing riders to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030784911/route-of-the-twelve-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030760620/sierra-de-la-calera-and-sierra-de-maria-andres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Calera and Sierra de María Andrés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering diverse scenery and a circular route.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Nogales?
A: Nogales offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Given its desert-adjacent climate, spring and fall generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for riding. Winters are mild, while summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable during those months.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions for a post-ride cool down or family activity?
A: Beyond the trails, the region offers several attractions for relaxation or family outings. Patagonia Lake State Park, about 30 minutes north, is great for fishing, boating, and birdwatching. Peña Blanca Lake also provides tranquil spots for fishing and camping. For more local information, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitnogalesaz.com/places-to-visit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnogalesaz.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Located between the Monsalud and Rozallana mountains, rolling along beautiful and picturesque trails combined with sigletracks, we are in the area of the route of the Twelve Apostles, planned by the Nature Classroom of the Popular University of Salvaleón and which owes its name to the existence of twelve hundred-year-old cork oaks (currently seven).
Rolling through the area of the Twelve Apostles, we come across this beautiful single track that we mountain bike lovers like so much.
Nice and picturesque path next to the Nogales reservoir dam, which was built in 1991 on the Nogales riverbed. It currently supplies the vast majority of nearby towns.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike the Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route typically takes around 2 hours and 44 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles route is classified as moderate. It requires a good level of physical fitness due to its length and elevation gain, but it's generally suitable for most skill levels. Some sections may involve uphill climbs.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a variety of terrain, including beautiful and scenic trails, rolling hills within the Sierra de Monsalud and Sierra de Rozallana, and enjoyable singletracks. A notable section is the Doce Apóstoles singletrack, which is particularly appealing for mountain biking enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What notable natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is known for its picturesque scenery and several key features. You'll encounter the Nogales Reservoir and the famous Route of the Twelve Apostles (Centenary Cork Oaks). These 'Twelve Apostles' refer to ancient cork oaks, originally twelve in number, that give the trail its name, though currently only seven remain.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles is a circular route, meaning it loops back to its starting point. This makes for a convenient and enjoyable ride without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, as a circular route, you would typically find parking near the starting point, often in or around the area of the Nogales Reservoir or a nearby village that serves as a common trailhead for local routes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs on this specific trail is not explicitly stated. However, in many natural areas in Spain, dogs are generally permitted on trails, especially if kept on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead for definitive rules.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Embalse de Nogales y ruta de los Doce Apóstoles mountain biking route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small portion of this route, approximately 6%, follows the Camino de Barcarrota a Nogales. This indicates it shares sections with other established paths in the area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain biking trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a helmet, water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and navigation tools. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also advisable depending on the season.

waypoints.name: Route of the Twelve Apostles (Centenary Cork Oaks)
